summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Democracy / anti-fascism, AI / privacy. The strongest items in this run are JD Vance due in Budapest to back Viktor Orbán’s election campaign – Europe live, Middle East crisis live: Trump says he is ‘not at all’ worried about possible war crimes as his deadline for Iran nears, and As Iran war exposes global dependence on fossil fuels, the biggest emitters are reaping the rewards.

mainItems.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says he is ‘not at all’ worried about possible war crimes as his deadline for Iran nears
mainItems.summary: Israel warns Iranians to immediately stop using trains or being near railway lines, saying it would ‘endanger’ their lives Here are some of the latest images coming in from the Middle East as the war continues in week six.

mainItems.title: Samsung’s Galaxy S27 ‘Pro’ could squeeze in between the Ultra and Plus phones
mainItems.summary: Samsung's S27 lineup, expected next year, might include a new member, the Galaxy S27 Pro, which would be a second premium option between Samsung's Ultra and Plus models, as first reported by ETNews.

mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: ‘Buffet Infinity’ Trailer – Unique Horror-Comedy Told Through Fake TV Commercials
mainItems.summary: Buffet Infinity has an unconventional trailer to prepare you for the film, which takes a retro-screenlife approach to tell a story through a series of faux TV commercials.
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: "Keeping teams together doesn't seem to matter to people, but for us it did": Star Wars Zero Company's director on what the industry can learn from XCOM 2
mainItems.summary: It seems that every week we're reporting on news of another wave of layoffs at a big game studio. In March alone, we saw 1000 staff let go at Epic Games , 124 at Eidos Montreal , and over 100 at Ubisoft's Red Storm Entertainment .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Reframing CS with CSTA IMPACT Fellow Karen Stoll
mainItems.summary: Karen Stoll has taught computer science at Blacksburg High School in Virginia for the last eight years. As co–head coach... The post Reframing CS with CSTA IMPACT Fellow Karen Stoll appeared first on Computer Science Teachers Association .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Inside 5 Rural Texas Districts That Together Set Students on Path to the Future
mainItems.summary: Each day, hundreds of rural south Texas high schoolers wake before sunrise to board vans that bump for miles over back roads, crossing ranch land and thickets of brush.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Building AI bots becomes the latest viral craze in China

mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Shingles can hit younger than you think. The vaccine can prevent excruciating pain

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.
